title: Econ 148: Analytical and statistical packages for economist 1
text:
Class schedule: Monday & Tuesday | 17:00 - 18:00  Laboratory schedule: Wednesday | 16:00 - 19:00  Instructor: Christopher Llones  e-mail: christopher.llones@vsu.edu.ph  Pre-requisites: Econ 115 - Econometrics  Course credits: 3 units  Number of hours: 2 hrs lectures and 3 hrs laboratory per week 

Course description






Introduction to survey research and survey data analysis using R programming.





Course objectives







Create a valid and reliable survey questionnaire and survey codebook.
Perform exploratory data analysis on survey data using R programming.
Perform Parametric and Non-Parametric Test on Means using R.
Perform correlation analysis and build a regression model explaining relationship of certain economic variables in R.
Perform regression analysis on limited dependent variables in R.
Perform various techniques of multivariate data analysis in the R statistical software.

title: Module 1: Survey research design
text:
Methods of data collection
Sampling desing in surveys
Measurement issues in survey research
Questionnaire construction
Basics of interviewing
Creating codebook
Data entry

title: Course outline
text:
Week
Topics
Lessons
Activities




Week 1-2
Foundation of academic writing

Overview of academic writing genres and their purposes.
Discussion of the key principles of academic writing.
Introduction to the structure of academic papers.




Week 3-4
Developing research methods

Formulating research topics and research questions.
Identifying relevant literature and conducting a literature review.
Developing a thesis statement and creating an outline for a research paper.




Week 5-6
Writing first darf

Writing effective introductions and conclusions.
Crafting clear and concise methods and results section.
Writing persuasive literature and discussions.
Using appropriate language and tone in academic writing.




Week 7-8
Revising and editing

Strategies for revising and editing academic writing.
Peer review and feedback exercises.
Using tools and resources for editing and proofreading.




Week 9-10
Navigating peer-review process

Overview of the peer-review process in academic publishing.
Selecting appropriate journals for submission.
Preparing manuscripts for submission.
Responding to reviewer feedback and revising manuscripts.




Week 11-12
Ethical considerations in publishing

Discussion of plagiarism, authorship, and other ethical issues in academic publishing.
Introduction to copyright and intellectual property.
Exploration of open access and alternative publishing models.




Week 13-14
Conference presentation and other scholarly outputs

Preparing and delivering effective conference presentations.
Writing conference abstracts and proposal.
Exploring other scholarly outputs.




Week 15-16
Final project presentations and discussion

Presentation of final research papers and other scholarly projects.
Discussion and feedback from peers and instructor.
Reflections on the course and future goals in academic writing and publishing.
